So once you have set up the dollar breakout scan and once you set up the dollar breakout chart template, the next thing is once you run the dollar breakout scan, how are you going to select the stocks which show up in that particular scan?
Now it's exactly the same as what you're going to do in 4% breakout.
And for that, what you need to remember is the same thing which you need to remember what we did in, essentially, 4% breakout.
And that is we used a framework called two Lynch to find stocks which show up in 4% breakout.
So let's review two Lynch.
Again, same thing as what we did in 4% breakout.
And what is the thing which you need to remember when you're looking at two linch?
The stock should not be up two days in a row.
Linearity of the first leg of the move.
The stock should have linearity.
The stock should have had strong buying coming in before it consolidates.
Young setup, that means you want to buy the first or second or third setup and not buy fourth, fifth, sixth, or seventh setup in a trending move.
N, which stands for narrower negative day prior to consolidation.
C, which is consolidation quality.
In consolidation quality, you want to see not big breakdown.
You want to see lower volume.
You want to see volatility compressing.
You want to see, like, very compact, orderly consolidation, not a choppy consolidation, and close near high.
So these are the same thing which we look for in a 4% breakout.
And when you apply that to a dollar breakout, you're going to get candidates like you're going to get candidate what you found in dollar, in 4% record.
